content.problemStatement:
Implement a class that finds the median from a data stream. The median is the middle value in an ordered integer list. If the size of the list is even, there is no middle value, and the median is the mean of the two middle values.


Implement the MedianFinder class as follows:MedianFinder() initializes the MedianFinder object.
void addNum(int num) adds the integer num to the data structure.
double findMedian() returns the median of all elements so far. Answers within 10-5 of the actual answer will be accepted.
constraints: At most 10^5 calls will be made to addNum and findMedian.
content.inputFormat: The input will be a sequence of operations. Each operation will be either 'addNum' with an integer argument or 'findMedian' with no arguments.
content.outputFormat: The output will be the median of the numbers added so far, rounded to 5 decimal places.

solution.approach: Use two heaps, a max heap to store the smaller half of the numbers and a min heap to store the larger half.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log N) for addNum and findMedian operations

code.Python3:
class MedianFinder:
    def __init__(self):
        # Initialize an empty list to store the numbers
        self.nums = []

    def addNum(self, num: int) -> None:
        # Add the number to the list
        self.nums.append(num)

    def findMedian(self) -> float:
        # Sort the list to find median
        self.nums.sort()

        # Get the size of list
        n = len(self.nums)

        # If odd number of elements, return middle
        if n % 2 == 1:
            return self.nums[n // 2]

        # If even, return average of two middle elements
        return (self.nums[n // 2 - 1] + self.nums[n // 2]) / 2.0
